focusPoints.label: Make the quality bar concrete with examples.
focusPoints.reason: What prior feedback changed in his day-to-day output, or stayed abstract. Could be a gap in interpretation, or just missing a clear “good vs not good” example—worth clarifying with specific artifacts and outcomes.

focusPoints.label: How Carlos defines “good work” right now.
focusPoints.reason: Whether he can articulate the bar for his level in a way you can observe and measure. At Junior in a performance conversation, shared definitions prevent feedback from feeling subjective and demotivating.

focusPoints.label: Where he feels blocked in growth.
focusPoints.reason: What he thinks is limiting his next step right now, and what would make progress feel real. Could be skill gaps, feedback loop timing, or unclear expectations—worth surfacing so you can pick one near-term practice.

focusPoints.label: Feedback landing — both directions.
focusPoints.reason: How critique conversations have been landing: whether his revisions actually reflect the feedback, and whether he’s getting the kind of specificity he needs. This keeps performance feedback actionable rather than just evaluative.

name: Carlos
notes: For Carlos, this performance conversation needs to make the quality bar concrete and usable. I want to understand whether prior feedback translated into behavior changes or stayed abstract. I also need to hear how he defines good work right now and where he feels blocked in growth. Without that shared baseline, feedback can feel subjective and demotivating. My goal is to align on specific expectations, examples, and one near-term development focus he can practice immediately.
